verses.text: God spoke all these words, saying: I am the LORD thy God, which have brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage. Guidance: The Ten Commandments open with rescue, not rules. The God who delivers you names Himself first; the law that follows is for a freed people, not a way of earning freedom.

God spoke all these words, saying: I am the LORD thy God, which have brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage. Guidance: The Ten Commandments open with rescue, not rules. The God who delivers you names Himself first; the law that follows is for a freed people, not a way of earning freedom.

Thou shalt have no other gods before me. Guidance: The first commandment is not about religious tolerance but about exclusive love. God will not share the throne of your heart with another. What competes for first place in your time, your money, your imagination?

Thou shalt not make any graven image. Thou shalt not bow down to them nor serve them — for I the LORD thy God am a jealous God, visiting iniquity to the third and fourth generation, showing mercy unto thousands of them that love Me. Guidance: God's jealousy is the seriousness of covenant. Idols are images we'd rather control than a God who looks back. Mercy stretches to thousands; consequence to only three or four.

Thou shalt not make any graven image. Thou shalt not bow down to them nor serve them — for I the LORD thy God am a jealous God, visiting iniquity to the third and fourth generation, showing mercy unto thousands of them that love Me. Guidance: God's jealousy is the seriousness of covenant. Idols are images we'd rather control than a God who looks back. Mercy stretches to thousands; consequence to only three or four.

Thou shalt not make any graven image. Thou shalt not bow down to them nor serve them — for I the LORD thy God am a jealous God, visiting iniquity to the third and fourth generation, showing mercy unto thousands of them that love Me. Guidance: God's jealousy is the seriousness of covenant. Idols are images we'd rather control than a God who looks back. Mercy stretches to thousands; consequence to only three or four.

Thou shalt not take the name of the LORD thy God in vain; for the LORD will not hold him guiltless that taketh his name in vain. Guidance: God's name is His character. Don't use it as decoration, magic, or weapon. Speak Him truthfully or do not speak Him at all.

Remember the sabbath day to keep it holy. Six days thou shalt labour; the seventh is the sabbath of the LORD — no work, by thee, thy children, thy servants, thy cattle, nor the stranger in thy gates. For in six days the LORD made heaven and earth and rested the seventh; therefore He hallowed the sabbath. Guidance: Sabbath is built into creation. God's people rest — including servants and foreigners. Mercy in rhythm is a sign we trust God to keep the world running while we sit.

Remember the sabbath day to keep it holy. Six days thou shalt labour; the seventh is the sabbath of the LORD — no work, by thee, thy children, thy servants, thy cattle, nor the stranger in thy gates. For in six days the LORD made heaven and earth and rested the seventh; therefore He hallowed the sabbath. Guidance: Sabbath is built into creation. God's people rest — including servants and foreigners. Mercy in rhythm is a sign we trust God to keep the world running while we sit.

Honour thy father and thy mother: that thy days may be long upon the land which the LORD thy God giveth thee. Guidance: The first commandment with a promise. Honoring parents is not only obedience in childhood — it includes care, respect, and remembrance into their old age and beyond.
Thou shalt not kill. Guidance: Hebrew distinguishes murder from killing — this is the prohibition of unjust killing. Every human bears God's image; ending one is an attack on the One who made them.

Thou shalt not commit adultery. Guidance: God protects covenant by protecting the bed. Marriage is the smallest unit of covenant faithfulness; betraying it teaches the heart to betray every other promise.
Thou shalt not steal. Guidance: God protects what He has given each person. Stealing is not only taking goods — it is denying that God provides for both you and your neighbor.

Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour. Guidance: The commandment is specifically about courtroom witness — but it stretches to gossip, slander, and the careless words that shape how others are seen.

Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour's house, his wife, his manservant, his maidservant, his ox, his ass, nor any thing that is thy neighbour's. Guidance: The tenth commandment moves inside. It is not enough to refrain from theft and adultery — God's people are called to address the desire that would steal or betray.

All the people saw the thunderings, lightnings, trumpet, mountain smoking; they trembled and stood afar off. They said to Moses: speak with us, but let not God speak, lest we die. Moses: fear not — God is come to prove you. The people stood afar; Moses drew near to the thick darkness where God was. Guidance: God's holiness made the people back away; Moses drew in. The same fire repels some and draws others. Which side will you stand?

The same fire repels some and draws others. Which side will you stand?

The LORD: thus shalt thou say unto the children of Israel — ye have seen that I have talked with you from heaven. Ye shall not make with Me gods of silver, neither shall ye make unto you gods of gold. Guidance: The first commandment is restated as Israel walks home from the mountain. The temptation to make God in metal will be tested very soon. God knows His own people.

The LORD: thus shalt thou say unto the children of Israel — ye have seen that I have talked with you from heaven. Ye shall not make with Me gods of silver, neither shall ye make unto you gods of gold. Guidance: The first commandment is restated as Israel walks home from the mountain. The temptation to make God in metal will be tested very soon. God knows His own people.

An altar of earth thou shalt make, sacrificing thy burnt offerings on it. If of stone, not of hewn stone — for if thou lift thy tool on it, thou hast polluted it. Neither shalt thou go up by steps unto Mine altar, that thy nakedness be not discovered. Guidance: The first altar God commissions is earth and untouched stone — nothing ornamented, nothing exposed. God is content with the plainest offering, given with modesty. Worship doesn't need to be fancy; it needs to be reverent.
